data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 coverage finding; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED — FULLY covered by THIS child:
- Parent AC "Outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) is surfaced in the structured batch outcome" → 2359 AC1 (surfaces all three in the structured outbound batch outcome + RECON: line, parity with inbound applier). FULLY.
- Parent AC "A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout in Rollout & rollback" → 2359 AC2 (test asserts computed>0 && applied==0 fails, reproducing the link drop) + 2359 AC3 (fail-loud invariant rolls out warn-first behind a flag; promotion to hard-fail and reversion to warn = flag flip, no other code change — exact match to parent Rollout & rollback). FULLY.
- Parent SC "A silent sub-op no-op (e.g. links computed but never applied) fails a test/canary instead of passing green" → 2359 AC2. FULLY.

CONTRIBUTED: parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract … zero change-detector tests added" via 2359 AC5 (canary asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings); parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" via 2359 AC4 (sync semantics unchanged, suite green).

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings, consistent with this child's declared OUT items (probe wiring → 822a; applier consolidation → 33d0): SSOT schema + fetcher-vs-schema + differ-reads-vs-schema + round-trip contract through production path with real fixtures + SC producer/consumers cannot drift → 8937 (+ fe57 fixtures/fake, 59ef verified-fake honesty); collapse-to-single-path + retired-path-deleted + SC production-path-exercised → 33d0; bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically + every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded + SC no-probe-encodes-a-bug → 822a.

ANTI-FP / WITHDRAWAL of the recurring "AC6 documented anti-change-detector guardrail only PARTIALLY covered" finding: it stays WITHDRAWN as a false positive. The parent AC reads verbatim "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" — it explicitly NAMES its owner (story B = sibling 8937) and is FULLY covered there (8937 description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact …" + 8937 AC5 "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note …) is committed and referenced by the new tests"). Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a named consumer counts), it is FULLY covered. Not re-filed.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY — parent "sub-op telemetry surfaced", parent "computed>0 && applied==0 fails loud (warn-first)", parent SC "silent no-op fails canary" (all owned by THIS child). CONTRIBUTED — parent SC "zero change-detector" + parent AC "suite green". COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — all schema/contract/round-trip/applier-collapse/probe/guardrail criteria (8937/fe57/59ef/33d0/822a). STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent AC "all six closed; suite green". UNCOVERED — none. No G3 coverage finding for this pairing.
